title: Melanoseris hastata
intro: Melanoseris hastata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of dandelion-like plant. The plant is traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective effects. The bioactive constituents of Melanoseris hastata include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Melanoseris hastata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Melanoseris hastata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Melanoseris and is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant is further classified into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Melanoseris hastata is also known by its synonyms, which include Crepis hastata and Phalacroseris hastata. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular studies.
